chunks.text: Aydın Buluç is a Senior Scientist at the Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (LBNL) and an Adjunct Associate Professor of EECS at UC Berkeley. His research interests include parallel computing, combinatorial scientific computing, high performance graph analysis and machine learning, sparse computations, computational biology. Previously, he was a Luis W. Alvarez postdoctoral fellow at LBNL and a visiting scientist at the Simons Institute for the Theory of Computing.
chunks.question: What are Aydın Buluç's main research interests?
chunks.answer: Parallel computing, combinatorial scientific computing, high performance graph analysis.

chunks.text: He received his PhD in Computer Science from the University of California, Santa Barbara in 2010 and his BS in Computer Science and Engineering from Sabanci University, Turkey in 2005. Dr. Buluç is a recipient of the DOE Early Career Award in 2013 and the IEEE TCSC Award for Excellence for Early Career Researchers in 2015. He was a founding associate editor of the ACM Transactions on Parallel Computing.
chunks.question: When did Aydın Buluç receive his PhD and from where?

chunks.text: He recently led a team that was chosen as a finalist for the 2022 ACM Gordon Bell Prize. He is currently directing a DOE Mathematical Multifaceted Integrated Capabilities Center named Sparsitute. Education: 2010, Ph.D., Computer Science, University of California, Santa Barbara.
chunks.question: What recent prize was Aydın Buluç's team a finalist for?

chunks.text: Research Areas: Scientific Computing (SCI), Programming Systems (PS), Biosystems & Computational Biology (BIO), Computer Architecture & Engineering (ARC). Research Centers: SpeciaLIzed Computing Ecosystems (SLICE).
chunks.question: What are Aydın Buluç's research areas at UC Berkeley?
chunks.answer: Scientific Computing, Programming Systems, Biosystems & Computational Biology.

chunks.text: Selected Publications: A. Tripathy, K. A. Yelick, and A. Buluç, 'Reducing Communication in Graph Neural Network Training,' in Proceedings of the 2020 ACM/IEEE International Conference for High Performance Computing, Networking, Storage and Analysis, SC'20, 2020. E. Georganas, R. Egan, S. Hofmeyr, E. Goltsman, B. Arndt, A. Tritt, A. Buluç, L. Oliker, and K. A. Yelick, 'Extreme scale de novo metagenome assembly,' in Proceedings of the International Conference for High Performance Computing, Networking, Storage, and Analysis (SC'18), 2018, pp. 10.
chunks.question: What is the title of Aydın Buluç's 2020 publication?
chunks.answer: Reducing Communication in Graph Neural Network Training.

chunks.text: A. Gholami, A. Azad, P. Jin, K. Keutzer, and A. chunks.question: What algorithm does HipMCL implement?

chunks.text: Y. You, A. Buluç, and J. Demmel, 'Scaling Deep Learning on GPU and Knights Landing Clusters,' in Proceedings of the International Conference for High Performance Computing, Networking, Storage and Analysis (SC'17), 2017. A. Azad, A. Buluç, and A. Pothen, 'Computing Maximum Cardinality Matchings in Parallel on Bipartite Graphs via Tree-Grafting,' IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems (TPDS), 2016.
chunks.question: What is the focus of the 2017 publication by You, Buluç, and Demmel?
chunks.answer: Scaling deep learning on GPU clusters.

chunks.text: Selected Honors and Awards: TCHPC Award for Excellence for Early Career Researchers in High Performance Computing, 2015. DOE Early Career Research Award, 2013.
chunks.question: What award did Aydın Buluç receive in 2015?
